 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3gvd","title":"Enhance Playwright reporter with metrics events","description":"## Priority 3: Enhance Playwright Reporter with Metrics Events

### Current Issue
The Playwright JSONL reporter does not emit metrics events for performance data captured during tests.

### Required Changes

1. **Add metrics event emission via test attachments:**

```typescript
// In jsonl-reporter.ts

onTestEnd(test: TestCase, result: TestResult) {
  // ... existing test_end logic ...
  
  // Check for metrics attachments
  for (const attachment of result.attachments) {
    if (attachment.name === 'metrics' && attachment.body) {
      const metricsData = JSON.parse(attachment.body.toString());
      this.emitEvent({
        event: 'metrics',
        name: metricsData.name || test.title,
        metrics: {
          duration_ms: metricsData.duration_ms,
          memory_bytes: metricsData.memory_bytes,
          throughput_per_sec: metricsData.throughput_per_sec,
          ...metricsData.custom
        },
        test: this.getTestInfo(test)
      });
    }
  }
}
```

2. **Update spec files to report metrics:**

```typescript
// Example in mobile/performance.spec.ts
test('measures page load performance', async ({ page }) => {
  const startTime = Date.now();
  
  await page.goto('/heavy-page.html');
  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
  
  const duration = Date.now() - startTime;
  const metrics = await page.evaluate(() => ({
    memory: (performance as any).memory?.usedJSHeapSize
  }));
  
  // Report metrics via attachment
  await test.info().attach('metrics', {
    body: JSON.stringify({
      name: 'page_load_heavy',
      duration_ms: duration,
      memory_bytes: metrics.memory
    }),
    contentType: 'application/json'
  });
});
```

### Files to Modify
- tests/e2e/reporters/jsonl-reporter.ts
- tests/e2e/mobile/performance.spec.ts

### Testing Requirements (CRITICAL)

1. **Unit tests for metrics emission:**
```typescript
describe('JsonlReporter metrics', () => {
  it('emits metrics event from attachment', () => {
    const reporter = new JsonlReporter();
    const events: any[] = [];
    reporter.emitEvent = (e) => events.push(e);
    
    const mockResult = {
      attachments: [{
        name: 'metrics',
        body: Buffer.from(JSON.stringify({
          name: 'test_metric',
          duration_ms: 100,
          memory_bytes: 1024
        }))
      }]
    };
    
    reporter.onTestEnd(mockTest, mockResult);
    
    expect(events).toContainEqual(expect.objectContaining({
      event: 'metrics',
      name: 'test_metric',
      metrics: expect.objectContaining({
        duration_ms: 100,
        memory_bytes: 1024
      })
    }));
  });
});
```

2. **Integration test:**
```bash
npx playwright test mobile/performance.spec.ts
cat test-results/e2e/playwright_*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics")'
# Should show metrics events
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Metrics event emitted from 'metrics' attachments
- [ ] Duration, memory, throughput fields supported
- [ ] Custom metrics fields passed through
- [ ] Events follow existing schema format
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] mobile/performance.spec.ts reports metrics","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:21:50.014864917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:42.626519348Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3gvd","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-17g6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:09.111517617Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3ix9","title":"[Task] Opt 6.4: Benchmark streaming canonicalization","description":"## Objective
Benchmark the performance improvement from streaming canonicalization.

## Benchmark Scenarios

### 1. Current Benchmark
`canonicalize_long_message`: 951µs baseline

### 2. Before/After Comparison
```rust
#[bench]
fn bench_canonicalize_old(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let text = load_long_message();
    b.iter(|| canonicalize_for_embedding(&text))
}

#[bench]
fn bench_canonicalize_streaming(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let text = load_long_message();
    b.iter(|| canonicalize_for_embedding_streaming(&text))
}
```

### 3. Allocation Profiling
Use jemalloc to measure:
- Total bytes allocated per call
- Number of allocations per call
- Peak heap usage

### 4. Input Size Scaling
- Short text (100 chars)
- Medium text (1000 chars)
- Long text (10000 chars)
- Very long text (MAX_EMBED_CHARS+)

## Success Criteria
- 951µs → ~300µs (3x speedup)
- Allocations reduced from 4+ to 2
- Total allocated bytes reduced by ~50%

## Documentation
- Record results in PLAN doc or PR description
- Include before/after flamegraphs if possible

## Parent Feature

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3koo","title":"Create comprehensive E2E logging acceptance test","description":"## Priority 1: Comprehensive E2E Logging Acceptance Test

### Purpose
Create a final acceptance test that verifies the ENTIRE E2E logging system works end-to-end, running all E2E tests with logging enabled and validating the aggregated output.

### Required Implementation

1. **Create acceptance test script (scripts/e2e_logging_acceptance_test.sh):**

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== E2E Logging Acceptance Test ==="
echo "This test verifies the entire E2E logging system works correctly."

# Clean previous results
rm -f test-results/e2e/*.jsonl

# Run all E2E tests with logging enabled
echo ""
echo "Step 1: Running E2E tests with logging..."
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test 'e2e_*' -- --test-threads=1 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e_test_output.txt
TEST_EXIT=$?

# Verify JSONL files were created
echo ""
echo "Step 2: Verifying JSONL files created..."
JSONL_COUNT=$(ls -1 test-results/e2e/*.jsonl 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
if [ "$JSONL_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "FAIL: No JSONL files created"
    exit 1
fi
echo "Found $JSONL_COUNT JSONL files"

# Validate all JSONL files
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Validating JSONL schema..."
cargo test --test e2e_jsonl_schema_test || {
    echo "FAIL: JSONL schema validation failed"
    exit 1
}

# Verify event coverage
echo ""
echo "Step 4: Checking event coverage..."

EVENTS=$(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq -r '.event' | sort -u)
REQUIRED_EVENTS="run_start test_start test_end run_end"

for event in $REQUIRED_EVENTS; do
    if ! echo "$EVENTS" | grep -q "^$event$"; then
        echo "FAIL: Missing required event type: $event"
        exit 1
    fi
done
echo "All required event types present"

# Verify phase events
echo ""
echo "Step 5: Checking phase event coverage..."
PHASE_COUNT=$(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "phase_end")' | wc -l)
if [ "$PHASE_COUNT" -lt 10 ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Only $PHASE_COUNT phase_end events found (expected > 10)"
else
    echo "Found $PHASE_COUNT phase_end events"
fi

# Verify metrics events
echo ""
echo "Step 6: Checking metrics coverage..."
METRICS_COUNT=$(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics")' | wc -l)
if [ "$METRICS_COUNT" -lt 5 ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Only $METRICS_COUNT metrics events found (expected > 5)"
else
    echo "Found $METRICS_COUNT metrics events"
fi

# Generate summary report
echo ""
echo "Step 7: Generating summary..."
echo "=== E2E Logging Summary ===" > test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Test execution exit code: $TEST_EXIT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "JSONL files: $JSONL_COUNT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Total events: $(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | wc -l)" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Event types: $(echo "$EVENTS" | tr '\n' ', ')" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Phase events: $PHASE_COUNT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Metrics events: $METRICS_COUNT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
cat test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt

echo ""
if [ $TEST_EXIT -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "=== ACCEPTANCE TEST PASSED ==="
else
    echo "=== ACCEPTANCE TEST COMPLETED WITH TEST FAILURES ==="
    echo "Note: Some E2E tests may have failed, but logging infrastructure is working"
fi
```

### Files to Create
- scripts/e2e_logging_acceptance_test.sh

### Dependencies
This task depends on:
- All PhaseTracker additions (-35pi, -yfcu, -wjuo, -vcig, -272x)
- All metrics additions (-2jpl, -154c, -5c15)
- JSONL schema validation test (-2u25)
- Shell logging helper (-20bz)

### Testing Requirements

1. **Run acceptance test:**
```bash
./scripts/e2e_logging_acceptance_test.sh
# Must exit 0
```

2. **Verify acceptance report:**
```bash
cat test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
# Should show reasonable counts for all metrics
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script runs all E2E tests with logging
- [ ] Script validates JSONL schema
- [ ] Script checks for required event types
- [ ] Script warns about missing phase/metrics coverage
- [ ] Script generates summary report
- [ ] Script exits 0 when logging infrastructure is working
- [ ] Report shows > 10 phase events
- [ ] Report shows > 5 metrics "2026-01-27T17:34:13.697063883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3koo","title":"Create comprehensive E2E logging acceptance test","description":"## Priority 1: Comprehensive E2E Logging Acceptance Test

### Purpose
Create a final acceptance test that verifies the ENTIRE E2E logging system works end-to-end, running all E2E tests with logging enabled and validating the aggregated output.

### Required Implementation

1. **Create acceptance test script (scripts/e2e_logging_acceptance_test.sh):**

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== E2E Logging Acceptance Test ==="
echo "This test verifies the entire E2E logging system works correctly."

# Clean previous results
rm -f test-results/e2e/*.jsonl

# Run all E2E tests with logging enabled
echo ""
echo "Step 1: Running E2E tests with logging..."
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test 'e2e_*' -- --test-threads=1 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e_test_output.txt
TEST_EXIT=$?

# Verify JSONL files were created
echo ""
echo "Step 2: Verifying JSONL files created..."
JSONL_COUNT=$(ls -1 test-results/e2e/*.jsonl 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
if [ "$JSONL_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "FAIL: No JSONL files created"
    exit 1
fi
echo "Found $JSONL_COUNT JSONL files"

# Validate all JSONL files
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Validating JSONL schema..."
cargo test --test e2e_jsonl_schema_test || {
    echo "FAIL: JSONL schema validation failed"
    exit 1
}

# Verify event coverage
echo ""
echo "Step 4: Checking event coverage..."

EVENTS=$(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq -r '.event' | sort -u)
REQUIRED_EVENTS="run_start test_start test_end run_end"

for event in $REQUIRED_EVENTS; do
    if ! echo "$EVENTS" | grep -q "^$event$"; then
        echo "FAIL: Missing required event type: $event"
        exit 1
    fi
done
echo "All required event types present"

# Verify phase events
echo ""
echo "Step 5: Checking phase event coverage..."
PHASE_COUNT=$(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "phase_end")' | wc -l)
if [ "$PHASE_COUNT" -lt 10 ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Only $PHASE_COUNT phase_end events found (expected > 10)"
else
    echo "Found $PHASE_COUNT phase_end events"
fi

# Verify metrics events
echo ""
echo "Step 6: Checking metrics coverage..."
METRICS_COUNT=$(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics")' | wc -l)
if [ "$METRICS_COUNT" -lt 5 ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Only $METRICS_COUNT metrics events found (expected > 5)"
else
    echo "Found $METRICS_COUNT metrics events"
fi

# Generate summary report
echo ""
echo "Step 7: Generating summary..."
echo "=== E2E Logging Summary ===" > test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Test execution exit code: $TEST_EXIT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "JSONL files: $JSONL_COUNT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Total events: $(cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | wc -l)" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Event types: $(echo "$EVENTS" | tr '\n' ', ')" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Phase events: $PHASE_COUNT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
echo "Metrics events: $METRICS_COUNT" >> test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt
cat test-results/e2e/acceptance_report.txt

echo ""
if [ $TEST_EXIT -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "=== ACCEPTANCE TEST PASSED ==="
else
    echo "=== ACCEPTANCE TEST COMPLETED WITH TEST FAILURES ==="
    echo "Note: Some E2E tests may have failed, but logging infrastructure is working"
fi
```
